title: Tracing, Evals, and Failure Analysis · Durable Agent Mission
summary: A high-stakes incident coordinator. The coordinator needs parallel evidence gathering with permissions, traceability, synthesis, and explicit stop conditions. Planned artifact: Deliver Human-Controlled Multi-Agent Investigation with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Place approvals immediately before consequential effects with enough evidence to decide.
Delegate only concrete independent work with a clear return contract.
Verify subagent claims and integrate evidence rather than concatenating outputs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.

steps.title: Defend the stakeholder delivery
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Trajectory Evals applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Evaluate action trajectories and state outcomes as well as final text.
